why: Prevent a compromised/typosquatted upstream :latest push from landing arbitrary code in every future agent session (Fable M5).
evidence: Top-level manifest-list digest resolved via `docker buildx imagetools inspect jpoley/daax-agents:latest` (Digest: sha256:2153f137...). Stays multi-arch (amd64+arm64). CLAUDE_CONTAINER_IMAGE override preserved. Existing VALID_IMAGE_NAME_PATTERN already accepts @sha256 refs; docker image inspect resolves the pulled digest so isImageAvailable works.
regression: tests/server/config/constants.test.ts asserts DEFAULT_CONTAINER_IMAGE matches /@sha256:[0-9a-f]{64}$/ and is not :latest.
refresh_script: scripts/refresh-agent-images.sh pulls the pinned digest by ref (content-addressed verification) and advisory-checks whether :latest drifted past the pin; a failed digest pull now fails the script.

what: Kept Claude/OpenCode auth mounts read-WRITE; documented accepted risk + follow-up at the mount site.
why: Write-back is REQUIRED: (1) Claude Code refreshes OAuth tokens and rewrites .claude/.credentials.json — :ro breaks auth as the token expires; (2) both tools persist the INITIAL login into the (initially empty) mounted dir via `claude login`/`opencode auth login` inside the container — :ro would make authentication impossible. Forcing :ro would break AC#4 (auth persistence).
evidence: server/docker/auth-paths.ts: dirs are created empty (mkdirSync) then populated by login inside the container; chown 1000:1000 + 'Persist auth' design confirms RW intent. Claude auth is a daax-DEDICATED store (~/.daax-claude host / ${workspace}/.daax/claude container), NOT the operator's real ~/.claude — the issue's premise overstated this for Claude. OpenCode host mode maps the real ~/.local/share/opencode; container mode isolates to ${workspace}/.daax/opencode.
residual_risk: Untrusted agent code can exfiltrate the long-lived token in the mounted store; store is shared across daax agent sessions.
followup: Per-session short-lived scoped tokens so a compromised session cannot exfiltrate a durable credential or read another session's token.

what: Left agent containers on --network daax-net; documented evaluated-and-deferred.
why: daax-net is the shared plane for daax's own services (code-server, MCP gateway) that agent sessions legitimately use; isolating agents risks breaking those integrations. Postgres exposure is mitigated separately by requiring a real DAAX_PG_PASSWORD in deploy compose (the `daax` default is local-throwaway only).
followup: Dedicated agent network with an explicit allow-list to just the services agents need.
